summary: Works On This Work rail dynamically avoids overlapping uploaded-video frames in wide/theater modes.
Uploaded/HLS videos keep native controls, desktop resize, desktop-only preview, and mobile preview disabled.
Works mobile list auto-appends and stops at the final page without Load more.
Study mobile browser-back restore is mobile-only; desktop width keeps desktop pagination/layout.
Blog excerpt persistence is manual-only while content text remains available for search indexing.
Study list cards show all saved tags.
WorkEditor client-side IDs fall back when crypto.randomUUID is unavailable, including existing Work edit screens with metadata and video controls.
E2E latency coverage explicitly measures desktop public nav clicks and Work/Study detail-card opens.
intentionallyNotChanged: YouTube embeds do not receive uploaded-video resize controls.
Historical videos without preview assets were not reprocessed.
Home recent-post cards and More Studies keep their existing tag-display policy.
Public API wire shapes were not changed.
Tablet compact lists keep manual Load more.

risks: Docker Desktop port publishing for nginx failed in this WSL session; Playwright used a no-port nginx container on the compose network.
npm run lint reports existing non-blocking warnings unrelated to this production path.
Backend restore/build emits an existing low-severity AWSSDK.Core advisory warning.
Desktop back after mobile reading prioritizes desktop layout stability over raw mobile scroll restoration.

npm run build was attempted during the crypto addendum but stopped after more than 4 minutes with no output on the /mnt/d workspace; local browser verification uses next dev on 0.0.0.0:3002 behind the 0.0.0.0:3000 proxy.
Intermittent stale public detail after edit was not reproduced in this addendum; save paths still await public revalidation before routing or refresh.
Current local click slowness is primarily next dev cold route compilation and generateStaticParams overhead; observed first compiles included /blog/[slug] around 6.8s and /works/[slug] around 8.7s.

The full Playwright suite was not run in this latency addendum because it lists 589 tests and the active server is next dev; this would measure dev compilation, video recording, and test-data setup overhead more than production app latency.
